name: Logstash
what_it_does: Open-source server-side data processing pipeline that ingests data from multiple sources, transforms it, and ships it to Elasticsearch or other outputs.
use_cases: Parse syslog, JSON, and CSV logs and index them into Elasticsearch for Kibana dashboards
Enrich log events with GeoIP data and user-agent parsing before indexing
Collect logs from Kafka topics and fan-out to multiple Elasticsearch clusters
Filter and redact PII fields (credit cards, SSNs) from logs before storage
Convert legacy log formats to structured JSON for downstream analytics pipelines
not_for: Lightweight edge log shipping — use Filebeat or Fluent Bit instead
Real-time CEP (complex event processing) with sub-10ms latency
Metrics collection — use Metricbeat or Prometheus
best_when: Already using the Elastic Stack and need a central processing pipeline with rich filter plugins.
avoid_when: Resource-constrained environments — Logstash JVM footprint exceeds 500MB baseline.

auth.notes: Self-hosted; TLS + X-Pack security configurable for Elasticsearch output

pricing.notes: Open source; Elastic offers commercial support and managed hosting

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted; JVM heap and pipeline workers are primary throughput levers

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Config errors shown at startup; runtime pipeline errors logged but can be silent
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Persistent queue provides at-least-once; Elasticsearch output deduplication via document IDs
known_agent_gotchas: JVM startup takes 10-30s — not suitable for short-lived agent processes
Pipeline reload (SIGHUP) does not always pick up all config changes without full restart
Grok pattern debugging requires external tooling — the error messages for mismatched patterns are minimal
Multiple pipelines feature can cause memory contention — monitor heap carefully
Dead letter queue accumulates silently if not monitored — events dropped without alerting by default

security_score_components.security_notes: JVM vulnerabilities require timely updates; keystore for secrets but config files still risk exposure
